diagnoses.name: Unstable angina pectoris secondary to atherosclerotic native coronary artery disease
diagnoses.evidence_span: Patient is a middle-aged adult with significant cardiac history presenting with unstable angina pectoris. The pt reported acute chest discomfort that was unresponsive to rest and sublingual nitroglycerin at home, prompting emergent evaluation. The patient has known atherosclerotic heart disease of native coronary arteries

diagnoses.name: Obesity
diagnoses.evidence_span: He carries diagnoses of obesity, hyperlipidemia, and anxiety disorder, which have complicated his medical management. BMI documented at 32.4 (obesity range). Obese male in mild distress.

diagnoses.name: Hyperlipidemia
diagnoses.evidence_span: He carries diagnoses of obesity, hyperlipidemia, and anxiety disorder. Lipid panel demonstrates elevated total cholesterol and LDL consistent with uncontrolled hyperlipidemia.

diagnoses.name: Anxiety disorder
diagnoses.evidence_span: He carries diagnoses of obesity, hyperlipidemia, and anxiety disorder, which have complicated his medical management. He reports significant anxiety about his cardiac condition. Patient has multiple cardiac risk factors including obesity (BMI 32.0-32.9), uncontrolled hyperlipidemia, nicotine dependence, and significant anxiety disorder.

diagnoses.name: Nicotine dependence
diagnoses.evidence_span: The pt is an active smoker with nicotine dependence and has struggled with smoking cessation despite counseling.

diagnoses.name: Family history of ischemic heart disease and other diseases of the circulatory system
diagnoses.evidence_span: strong family history of ischemic heart disease and other circulatory disorders. Strong family history of ischemic heart disease places him at elevated ongoing risk.

diagnoses.name: Hyperglycemia
diagnoses.evidence_span: Glucose initially 168 mg/dL; managed with insulin infusion protocol.

procedures.name: Cardiac catheterization
procedures.evidence_span: Cardiac catheterization confirmed significant coronary artery stenosis.

procedures.name: Coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG) with left internal mammary artery to left anterior descending artery
procedures.evidence_span: Coronary artery bypass grafting was performed with left internal mammary artery to left anterior descending artery via open surgical approach.

procedures.name: Continuous cardiac output monitoring
procedures.evidence_span: Continuous cardiac output monitoring was maintained perioperatively to assess hemodynamic status and guide fluid and vasopressor management.

medications.name: Clopidogrel
medications.evidence_span: Continue aspirin 81 mg daily and clopidogrel 75 mg daily for dual antiplatelet therapy given coronary graft.

medications.name: Metoprolol
medications.evidence_span: Beta-blocker management with metoprolol titrated to HR 60-70 bpm and SBP >100 mmHg.

medications.name: Nitroglycerin sublingual
medications.evidence_span: Sublingual nitroglycerin available for anginal symptoms.

medications.name: Rosuvastatin
medications.evidence_span: Rosuvastatin 40 mg daily for aggressive lipid management.

medications.name: Acetaminophen
medications.evidence_span: Continue acetaminophen and opioid analgesics (oxycodone, hydromorphone) as needed for post-operative pain.

medications.name: Oxycodone
medications.evidence_span: Continue acetaminophen and opioid analgesics (oxycodone, hydromorphone) as needed for post-operative pain.

medications.name: Hydromorphone
medications.evidence_span: Continue acetaminophen and opioid analgesics (oxycodone, hydromorphone) as needed for post-operative pain.

medications.name: Diazepam
medications.evidence_span: Diazepam incorporated for anxiety management given documented anxiety disorder.

medications.name: Gabapentin
medications.evidence_span: Gabapentin 300 mg daily for neuropathic symptom relief.

medications.name: Docusate
medications.evidence_span: Continue bowel regimen including docusate, senna, lactulose, and polyethylene glycol given opioid use.

medications.name: Senna
medications.evidence_span: Continue bowel regimen including docusate, senna, lactulose, and polyethylene glycol given opioid use.

medications.name: Lactulose
medications.evidence_span: Continue bowel regimen including docusate, senna, lactulose, and polyethylene glycol given opioid use.

medications.name: Polyethylene glycol
medications.evidence_span: Continue bowel regimen including docusate, senna, lactulose, and polyethylene glycol given opioid use.

medications.name: Nicotine patch
medications.evidence_span: Nicotine patch 21 mg transdermal daily to address nicotine dependence.

medications.name: Insulin
medications.evidence_span: Glucose initially 168 mg/dL; managed with insulin infusion protocol.

chief_complaint: Unstable angina pectoris with acute chest discomfort unresponsive to rest and sublingual nitroglycerin at home
